drinks.name: House Negroni
drinks.description: The house standard. Ford's gin, Cinzano Rosso, and Campari in perfect balance — stirred until cold, strained over a single cube, finished with a lemon peel.
drinks.tastingNotes: Bittersweet orange, botanical gin, bright lemon oil finish
recipe: Combine all ingredients in a mixing glass filled with ice.
Stir over ice until properly chilled and diluted — about 30 seconds.
Strain over a single large ice cube in a rocks glass.
Express a lemon peel over the glass and use as garnish.

drinks.name: White Mezcal Negroni
drinks.tagline: A pale, smoky deviation from the classic
drinks.description: Mezcal steps in for gin, Luxardo Bitter Bianco replaces Campari, and Dolin Dry stands in for sweet vermouth. The result is pale, aromatic, and faintly smoky.
drinks.tastingNotes: Smoky mezcal, bitter white citrus, dry herbal vermouth, mineral finish
ingredients: 1 oz Mezcal (400 Conejos or Alipús; Espadín or Tobalá)
recipe: Combine all ingredients in a mixing glass with ice.
Stir until well-chilled.
Strain over a large cube in a rocks glass.
Express a lemon peel over the surface and add as garnish.

drinks.name: Vesper Martini
drinks.tagline: Shaken, not stirred — just this once
drinks.description: Three parts gin, one part vodka, half a part Lillet Blanc. Shaken hard and strained cold. The drink that James Bond ordered once, and cocktail bars have been arguing about ever since.
drinks.tastingNotes: Botanical gin, clean vodka, faint orange floral from Lillet, bone dry
ingredients: 3 oz London Dry Gin or Old Tom Gin (Beefeater or Ford's)
recipe: Chill a coupe glass in the freezer for at least 15 minutes.
Combine gin, vodka, and Lillet Blanc in a shaker with ice.
Shake hard until very cold — about 12 seconds.
Fine strain through a mesh strainer into the chilled coupe.
Express a lemon peel over the surface and add as garnish.

drinks.name: House Dirty Martini
drinks.description: A full ounce of olive brine cuts through the gin and vermouth, creating something briny, botanical, and unapologetically savory. Stirred. Garnished with olives and lemon peel.
drinks.tastingNotes: Briny, botanical gin, dry vermouth, clean saline finish
ingredients: 2½ oz London Dry Gin or Old Tom Gin (Beefeater or Ford's)
recipe: Chill a coupe or martini glass in the freezer.
Combine gin, dry vermouth, and olive brine in a mixing glass with ice.
Stir until well-chilled — this is a stirred drink.
Strain into the chilled glass.
Garnish with olives and a lemon peel.

drinks.name: Perfect Manhattan
drinks.tagline: Rye and two vermouths, balanced precisely
drinks.description: Death & Co.'s version uses both sweet and dry vermouth in equal measure, building a Manhattan that is more complex and less cloying than the standard. A serious drink for serious company.
drinks.tastingNotes: Rye spice, dark cherry, balanced sweet and dry vermouth, Angostura warmth
recipe: Combine rye, both vermouths, and bitters in a mixing glass with ice.
Stir for 30 seconds until well-chilled.
Strain into a chilled coupe.
Garnish with a cherry.

drinks.name: Boulevardier
drinks.description: Rittenhouse Rye steps in for gin. The result is warmer, spicier, and unmistakably autumnal. Stirred and served over a single large cube.
drinks.tastingNotes: Rye spice, bitter orange, dark cherry, caramel warmth
recipe: Combine all ingredients in a mixing glass with ice.
Stir over ice until properly chilled.
Strain over a large cube in a rocks glass.
Express an orange peel over the glass and add as garnish.

drinks.name: Sazerac
drinks.description: Death & Co.'s version of a recipe that has not needed improvement since the 1800s. Rye, demerara, Peychaud's bitters, and an absinthe-rinsed glass. Served neat — no ice.
drinks.tastingNotes: Rye spice, anise, cherry-clove bitters, faint absinthe ghost
recipe: Chill a rocks glass, then pour a small amount of absinthe into it, swirl to coat, and discard the excess.
In a mixing glass, combine rye, demerara syrup, and Peychaud's bitters with ice.
Stir until well-chilled.
Strain into the absinthe-rinsed glass — no ice.
Express a lemon peel over the glass and rest on the rim.

drinks.name: House Carajillo
drinks.tagline: Coffee, citrus, and a cloud of smoke
drinks.description: Licor 43 and two shots of fresh espresso, shaken cold and strained over ice. Orange and cardamom bitters add depth. Optionally smoked with allspice, cinnamon, and star anise for a theatrical finish.
drinks.tastingNotes: Vanilla, fresh espresso, citrus bitters, warm baking spice
recipe: Pull two espresso shots and allow to cool slightly.
Combine Licor 43, espresso, and both bitters in a shaker with ice.
Shake hard for 12 seconds.
Strain over fresh ice in a rocks glass.
Optional: before adding the drink, smoke the glass with allspice, cinnamon, and star anise using a smoking gun or torch.

drinks.name: Bee's Knees
drinks.description: A Prohibition-era classic. Honey syrup and fresh lemon juice bring the gin into balance in a cocktail that is effortlessly elegant and dangerously easy to drink.
drinks.tastingNotes: Floral honey, bright lemon, botanical gin, clean dry finish
ingredients: ¾ oz Honey Syrup (1:1 honey to warm water)
recipe: Make honey syrup by combining equal parts honey and warm water; stir until dissolved, then cool.
Chill a coupe glass in the freezer.
Combine gin, fresh lemon juice, and honey syrup in a shaker with ice.
Shake well and strain into the chilled coupe.
Garnish with a lemon twist if desired.

drinks.name: Sidecar
drinks.tagline: Cognac's finest moment in a coupe
drinks.description: Cognac, orange liqueur, and fresh lemon juice shaken to order. The sugar rim is optional — the drink is balanced without it, but some nights call for it.
drinks.tastingNotes: Cognac stone fruit, bright orange liqueur, tart lemon, long finish
ingredients: ¾ oz Orange Liqueur (Cointreau or Grand Marnier)
recipe: Optional: rim the coupe with sugar by moistening the edge with lemon and dipping in fine sugar.
Combine cognac, orange liqueur, and fresh lemon juice in a shaker with ice.
Shake well until cold.
Strain into the prepared coupe.
Garnish with an orange twist.

drinks.name: Paloma
drinks.description: Tequila, fresh lime, and good grapefruit soda. Built over ice and barely stirred. The simplest drink on the menu, and one of the most refreshing things you can pour on a warm evening.
drinks.tastingNotes: Fresh grapefruit, agave, bright lime, effervescent and clean
ingredients: Grapefruit Soda to top (Jarritos or Fever-Tree)
recipe: Optional: rim the glass with salt.
Fill a highball glass with ice.
Add tequila and fresh lime juice.
Top with grapefruit soda.
Give it a light stir once to combine.
Garnish with a grapefruit wedge.

drinks.name: French 75
drinks.tagline: Gin and Champagne, combined without apology
drinks.description: Shaken gin, fresh lemon, and simple syrup topped with Champagne. Named for a World War I artillery piece. Drinks nothing like one.
drinks.tastingNotes: Botanical gin, bright lemon, effervescent, celebratory
ingredients: Champagne or dry Prosecco to top
recipe: Combine gin, fresh lemon juice, and simple syrup in a shaker with ice.
Shake well.
Strain into a champagne flute.
Top slowly with cold Champagne.
Garnish with a lemon twist.

drinks.name: Vieux Carré
drinks.description: Rye and Cognac share the glass with sweet vermouth, Bénédictine, and two bitters. Complex, layered, and best sipped slowly. A drink that rewards patience.
drinks.glassware: Rocks glass over a large cube
drinks.tastingNotes: Rye spice, cognac fruit, herbal Bénédictine, layered bitters
recipe: Combine all ingredients in a mixing glass with ice.
Stir until well-chilled.
Strain over a large cube in a rocks glass.
Express a lemon peel over the glass and add as garnish.

drinks.name: Peated Sour
drinks.tagline: Smoke, citrus, and a cloud of silk
drinks.description: Ardbeg Wee Beastie brings peat and intensity; fresh lemon and simple syrup bring balance; egg white brings structure. Finished with Angostura bitters on the foam. A serious drink.
drinks.tastingNotes: Peated smoke, bright lemon, light sweetness, silky egg white, long finish
recipe: Combine Scotch, lemon juice, simple syrup, and egg white in a shaker without ice.
Dry shake vigorously for 15 seconds to build foam.
Add ice and shake again until very cold.
Double strain through a fine mesh strainer into a chilled coupe.
Dot the foam with Angostura bitters and drag a toothpick through to create a pattern.

drinks.name: Paper Plane
drinks.description: A modern classic from Sam Ross at Milk & Honey. Bourbon, Amaro Nonino, Aperol, and fresh lemon juice in precisely equal measure. Balanced, bittersweet, and impossible to put down.
drinks.tastingNotes: Bitter orange Aperol, amaro herbs, bourbon warmth, bright citrus
ingredients: ¾ oz Bourbon or Rye Whiskey
recipe: Combine all four ingredients in equal measure in a shaker with ice.
Shake well.
Strain into a chilled coupe.
No garnish.
